subtitle: Pain at the base of the big toe

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Sesamoid stress more common in forefoot runners and sprinters

mild.description: Aching under the big toe joint after runs, worse in thin-soled shoes
mild.can_run_note: Yes — add sesamoid padding and use cushioned shoes
why: Sesamoid bones absorbing high load during toe-off phase
Low-drop or minimal shoes increasing sesamoid pressure
Forefoot running pattern concentrating load under the first metatarsal
relief: Sesamoid offloading pad — donut-shaped pad with a hole under the sesamoid
Stiff-soled shoe to limit big toe dorsiflexion
Ice under the big toe base for 15 min
prevention: Avoid prolonged barefoot activity on hard surfaces
Reduce forefoot running intensity if sesamoid-prone

moderate.description: Persistent big toe pain, tender to press, pain with push-off
moderate.can_run_note: Easy runs with stiff-soled shoe and sesamoid offloading pad only
why: Sesamoiditis — inflammation of the sesamoid bones and surrounding tissue
Bipartite sesamoid being stressed as if fractured
Hallux flexor tendinopathy contributing to local pain
relief: Sesamoid offloading orthotic — custom is most effective
prevention: Custom orthotics with sesamoid relief for high-mileage running
Footwear with stiff forefoot to limit dorsiflexion stress

severe.description: Severe big toe base pain, possible swelling, cannot push off
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible sesamoid stress fracture
why: Sesamoid stress fracture — slow to heal, can become avascular necrosis
Complete sesamoiditis requiring offloading for 6–8 weeks
Surgical removal may be needed in recalcitrant cases
relief: X-ray and bone scan — X-ray alone can miss sesamoid fractures
prevention: 8–12 week offloading and gradual return protocol
Permanent sesamoid offloading orthotic for future running

see_physio_if: Pain directly under the big toe base, tender to firm press
Pain that does not improve with cushioning and rest
Swelling visible under the big toe
